headline: SEVERE SLOP WARNING: SYNTHETIC TIDE CROSSES THE FIFTY-PERCENT HIGH-WATER MARK ACROSS THE OPEN INTERNET
advisory: The Service has confirmed that synthetic content now occupies more than half of all measured internet volume, placing human-authored material below the historic waterline for the first time on record. Residents are advised that the shoreline has not moved — the water has simply risen to meet it. Cling to bylines, timestamps, and anything with a verifiable street address. This is the Service's first bulletin issued while technically a minority of the medium it is printed on.

regional_outlook.conditions: Standing water on page one as freshly generated explainers pool faster than they can drain. Boil all 'top 10' results before consumption.
Dense fog of identical replies rolling in from coordinated low-pressure systems. Visibility into who is actually a person: under one meter.
Flash floods of narrated science 'facts' delivered in a calm synthetic baritone. Educational signage in the region is no longer load-bearing.
Brackish conditions as personalized outreach mixes with the spawning newsletter run. Salinity high, sincerity low.
Sediment of 200-page summaries-of-summaries accumulating on the seabed. Sandbars now visible at the $2.99 price point.

specimen.status: Invasive; now flocking in industrial numbers
specimen.habitat: Reply threads, public comment portals, and the bottom of any post with the word 'urgent' in it
specimen.field_marks: Generic regional avatar, account created six weeks ago, and a strangely heartfelt opinion delivered in the exact cadence of nine hundred other strangely heartfelt opinions. Plumage suspiciously well-lit.
specimen.call: As a longtime local resident and a concerned everyday citizen, I simply have to say what we are all already thinking.
specimen.confusion_species: Easily mistaken for an actual neighbor. Tell them apart by asking a follow-up question — the genuine article will ramble, complain about parking, and go off-topic; the Phantom Constituent will repeat its opening sentence with the nouns swapped.

spotter_tip: When a hundred accounts agree word-for-word, you are not witnessing consensus — you are witnessing one source wearing a hundred coats. Count the coats, not the heads.
tomorrow: Synthetic tide holding above the waterline; scattered authenticity possible near hand-edited footnotes by late afternoon.

confirmed_sighting.report: A new data analysis reports that more than half of all content on the internet is now AI-generated 'slop,' raising concerns about the dilution of human-made material online. The Service notes the figure with professional calm and a faint sense of personal involvement.
